programs.name: KanCare Medicaid
programs.what_it_does: KanCare is Kansas's Medicaid program providing health coverage including long-term services for seniors and disabled individuals. It covers medical care, nursing home services, and coordinates with waivers for home-based care.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ and disabled individuals meeting income and asset limits, at risk of institutionalization.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers medical costs, long-term care up to full scope based on needs[1][3]

programs.name: KanCare Frail Elderly Waiver (FE Waiver)
programs.what_it_does: The FE Waiver provides home and community-based services to help seniors avoid nursing home placement. Services include adult day care, personal care, emergency response systems, medical equipment, and wellness monitoring via managed care organizations.
programs.who_its_for: Kansas residents aged 65+ at risk of institutionalization, meeting financial and functional eligibility; limited slots with waitlist.
programs.benefit_value: Customized services based on needs; no fixed dollar amount, provided through MCO networks[1]

programs.name: KanCare PACE
programs.what_it_does: PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly) offers comprehensive medical and social services to eligible seniors in community settings. It includes adult day health care, transportation, home care, and hospital care coordination to support aging in place.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified as nursing facility eligible, able to live safely in community, meeting Medicaid/Medicare criteria.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitated coverage of Medicare and Medicaid services; value varies by participant needs[3]

programs.name: KanCare Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copays for eligible low-income seniors. QMB covers Part A/B premiums and costs; SLMB covers Part B; QI covers Part B for higher incomes.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ with Medicare, low income/asset limits (e.g., QMB: 100% FPL, SLMB: 120%, QI: 135%).
programs.benefit_value: QMB: full Part A/B costs; SLMB: $185.90/mo Part B (2026); QI: Part B premium[3]

programs.name: Food Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing food at stores. Helps low-income households afford nutritious meals.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+, households at or below 130-200% FPL depending on deductions; automatic eligibility if receiving SSI.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per senior household; max varies by size/income[3][7]

programs.name: Low-Income Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP provides grants to help pay heating/cooling bills and crisis assistance. Administered through local agencies for utility costs.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors at or below 150% FPL, priority for elderly/disabled.
programs.benefit_value: Grants $200-1,000 annually depending on fuel type, income, household size[3]

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs to reduce utility bills. Improves home safety and comfort for low-income residents.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households at or below 200% FPL, priority for seniors, disabled, families with children.
programs.benefit_value: Free services worth $5,000-10,000 per home in upgrades[3]

programs.name: Senior Health Insurance Counseling for Kansas (SHICK)
programs.what_it_does: SHICK offers free, unbiased counseling on Medicare, supplements, and other insurance options. Trained volunteers assist with claims, appeals, and enrollment.
programs.who_its_for: Kansas seniors 65+, younger disabled on Medicare, caregivers; no income limit.
programs.benefit_value: Free counseling saving thousands in improper premiums/claims[6]

programs.name: Kansas Senior Nutrition Program (Meals on Wheels)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers congregate and home-delivered meals to promote nutrition and social engagement. Part of Older Americans Act services through area agencies on aging.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, homebound or at nutrition risk; suggested donation.

programs.name: Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite care, training, counseling, and supplies to support family caregivers. Helps caregivers continue providing care while preventing burnout.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors 60+, or grandparents raising children; income-based.
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $1,500/year; varies by service[6]

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time community service jobs with training to low-income seniors for skill-building and employment transition. Pays minimum wage for 20 hours/week.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors 55+ unable to find work; priority for hardest-to-serve.

programs.name: Legal Services for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal aid for issues like benefits, housing, abuse, and consumer problems. Operates through Legal Aid of Kansas and elder law programs.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ facing civil legal needs; priority for vulnerable.
programs.benefit_value: Free representation saving $5,000+ in legal fees per case[7]

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care. Provides education and rights protection.
programs.who_its_for: Residents of nursing homes, assisted living in Kansas; families.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy resolving 80%+ complaints without litigation[6]
